name: nip-scope-matching
description: NID `scope.nodes` and `scope.actions` matching against an inbound request. `scope.nodes` entries are URL patterns with `*` glob (matches any single path segment) and `**` (matches across segments); `scope.actions` entries are exact-string capability tokens. A node/action MUST be covered by at least one entry, otherwise the request is rejected with `NWP-AUTH-NID-SCOPE-VIOLATION`.

vectors.description: Exact-prefix glob match: scope grants `nwp://api.myapp.com/*`; request to `nwp://api.myapp.com/products/query` matches because `*` covers the remaining path.
Cross-host rejection: scope only grants `nwp://api.myapp.com/*`; request targets `nwp://api.evil.com/products/query`. Different host → no match.
Action not in capability list: scope grants `orders:read` but request asks for `orders:create`. MUST be rejected.
Multi-pattern union: scope grants two distinct prefixes; request matches the second.

Token-budget exhaustion is a runtime check, not a scope-pattern match. Even with a scope match the request MUST be rejected when CGN budget is exceeded — this proves SDKs treat `max_token_budget` as a separate gate, not as part of the URL/action match.
